title: IREN Secures $9.7bn AI Cloud Contract with Microsoft
text: IREN Limited has announced a significant multi-year partnership with Microsoft involving GPU cloud services. Under this agreement, the company will provide Microsoft with access to NVIDIA GB300 GPUs over a five-year period, valued at approximately $9.7 billion including a 20% prepayment. To support this contract, IREN has also secured a separate agreement with Dell Technologies to procure the necessary GPUs and related equipment for roughly $5.8 billion. The hardware deployment will occur in phases throughout 2026 at IREN's Childress, Texas facility, which features 750MW of power capacity. The facility will include new liquid-cooled data centers capable of supporting 200MW of critical IT load across multiple horizons. Regarding funding, IREN plans to finance these capital expenditures through a combination of existing cash reserves, customer prepayments, operating cash flows, and additional financing arrangements. Co-CEO Daniel Roberts stated the partnership demonstrates IREN's position as a trusted provider of AI Cloud services and validates the strength of their vertically integrated platform. Microsoft's Jonathan Tinter praised IREN's integrated approach and secured power capacity, calling the company a strategic partner. IREN positions itself as a leading AI Cloud Service Provider specializing in large-scale GPU deployments for artificial intelligence applications across North America.
